title: SEDENA Officially Denies El Guano Capture; El Chapo's Brother Evades Major Federal Operation for Fourth Time
detail: Mexico's Defense Ministry (SEDENA) officially denied on April 23, 2026 that Aureliano 'El Guano' Guzmán Loera was among those captured in the previous day's federal operation in Badiraguato and the Golden Triangle — confirming he remained at large. García Harfuch had confirmed 'detainees' on April 22, but their identities were withheld. The five-helicopter operation — involving Black Hawks, Beechcraft T-6s, National Guard, and Marine elements — represented one of the largest federal security deployments into the Golden Triangle in recent memory, yet the primary target escaped. An Infobae follow-up on April 23 documented the strategic significance of the Triángulo Dorado as the zone El Guano controls, and his documented pattern of evading previous federal interdiction attempts. El Guano defected from Los Chapitos to support La Mayiza shortly after the September 9, 2024 offensive, becoming a significant asset in the sierra zone with control over both drug production and fuel-theft (huachicol) revenue. His continued presence at large in the Golden Triangle underscores the limits of federal enforcement in the most rugged terrain of the Sinaloa theater. Latin Times confirmed he remained at large based on official statements from Sinaloa authorities.

media.caption: SEDENA denies El Guano was captured in Badiraguato operation — El Chapo's brother remains at large

title: 10 'Gente del Guano' Associates Arrested in Tamazula Follow-Up Operation; El Guano's Right-Hand Man Captured
detail: A joint Mexican Army (SEDENA) and Navy (SEMAR) operation on April 23, 2026 — described by El Financiero as 'the second attempt to capture El Guano' — arrested 10 members of the 'Gente del Guano' faction in Tamazula, a mountainous locality on the Sinaloa-Durango border and a key stronghold for Aureliano 'El Guano' Guzmán Loera, El Chapo's brother. The detained included El Guano's right-hand man (identified only as 'Abel N'), his chief bodyguard, and his principal financial and logistics operator — effectively the operational command structure below El Guano himself. Weapons, ammunition, and explosive devices were seized in the operation. Latinus confirmed that the operation had US intelligence support. Despite the capture of his inner circle, El Guano himself escaped — marking the fourth time he has evaded federal interdiction. The operation demonstrated a shift from the broad April 22 Badiraguato air-and-ground operation to a more precise intelligence-driven strike in Tamazula. El Guano defected from the Chapitos after El Mayo's capture in July 2024 to join La Mayiza, becoming the dominant sierra-zone commander in the Golden Triangle. His control of both drug production and fuel-theft (huachicol) networks in the Badiraguato-Tamazula corridor makes him one of the highest-value remaining Sinaloa Cartel targets with a $5 million US State Department reward on his capture.

media.caption: 10 Gente del Guano members including El Guano's right-hand man arrested in Tamazula follow-up operation
